title: Ce am vrut să zic când am rupt diploma
summary: Political scientist Cristian Preda explains why he tore up a copy of his French doctoral diploma on live TV at Digi24, as a protest against the devaluation of doctoral degrees in Romania. He identifies three causes: inflation of doctoral theses driven by salary bonuses, lack of institutional authority to combat fraud, and the government's failure to distinguish between legitimate and plagiarized doctorates.
talking_points: Doctoral degrees in Romania have been devalued by an inflation of theses, driven by salary bonuses across all public sectors, not just academia
Universities and the Ministry of Education enabled the crisis by funding institutions based on doctoral enrollment numbers and failing to punish plagiarism
The extreme case of Nicolae Ciucă, who used his position as Prime Minister to block investigation of his plagiarized thesis through the Constitutional Court
The current government's decision to cut doctoral bonuses indiscriminately punishes honest young academics alongside fraudsters
Preda's protest was met with solidarity from some, but also with hostility, personal attacks, and accusations from journalists and anonymous social media users
author: Cristian Preda
